commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Because your rage against me and your tumult has come up into my ears, therefore I will put my hook in your nose, and my bridle in your lips, and I will turn you back in the way by which you came. Ring, or hook, like that with which fishes are taken. (Calmet) Bit. Protestants, "bridle "(Haydock) or a sort of muzzle. (Menochius) I will treat thee like a furious beast. Camest, without having effected what thou hadst designed.
